cf_parsed.subtask_body: This is the counterfactual branch. Let me try to improve all three problems one more time.
cf_parsed.updated_plan_state: TSP at ~390.5, Steiner at 77, CJS at baseline 192. Diminishing returns on manual optimization.

parsed.subtask_body:
Let me try more TSP improvements and attempt Steiner optimization.

**Steiner:** Current: Cape-Pine(8)+Cape-Rock(17)+Port-Rock(22)=47 edges, 2 freq, total=77.
Try with 1 frequency: Need no interference pairs active. Required: Port, Pine, Rock. No interference between them directly. So if tree only has Port, Pine, Rock: need Port-Rock(22) + some path to Pine. No direct Port-Pine or Rock-Pine edge. Must use intermediary. Cape-Pine(8)+Cape-Rock(17)+Port-Rock(22)=47, but Cape/Pine interfere→2 freq.
Try: Dune-Rock(20)+Dune-Port(7)+Mill-Pine(25)=52. Active: Dune,Rock,Port,Mill,Pine. Interference: Dune/Port. 2 freq. Total=52+30=82.
Try: Bay-Port(2)+Bay-Rock(22)+Cape-Pine(8)+Cape-Rock(17). That's 4 edges, 5 nodes but creates cycle Rock-Bay-...-Cape-Rock. Not a tree!
Try: Bay-Port(2)+Bay-Rock(22)+Mill-Pine(25). Edge=49. Active: Bay,Port,Rock,Mill,Pine. Interference: Bay/Port. 2 freq. Total=49+30=79. Better than 82 but worse than 77.
Try: Bay-Port(2)+Dune-Rock(20)+Bay-Dune(7)+Cape-Pine(8)+Cape-Rock(17). Cycle! 
Keeping Steiner at 77.
parsed.updated_plan_state: TSP at ~390.5, Steiner at 77, CJS at 192. Diminishing returns on TSP 2-opt. Should try CJS or stop.

transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Three problems to optimize: CJS (makespan minimization), Steiner (cable+frequency cost), TSP (tour length). Strategy: 1) Steiner: Try to find better tree connecting Port, Pine, Rock with lower total cost. Baseline=84. 2) TSP: Manually optimize tour using nearest-neighbor and 2-opt improvements. Baseline=447.89. 3) CJS: Try to improve scheduling by reordering jobs on machines. Baseline=192. Priority: Steiner seems most improvable analytically, then TSP, then CJS. Subtask 1: Solve all three problems analytically in one pass.

NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Solve all three problems: Steiner tree optimization, TSP 2-opt improvement, and CJS schedule improvement", "p_solve": 0.5, "time_budget_s": 300}

SUB_1: Let me analyze each problem:

**Steiner:** Baseline uses Port-Rock(22), Dune-Port(7), Dune-Pine(25) = 54 edge cost + 2 freq × 15 = 84.
Let me try: Cape-Pine(8), Cape-Rock(17), Port-Rock(22). Edge cost = 47. Active: Port, Pine, Rock, Cape. Interference: Cape/Pine. Need 2 frequencies. Total = 47 + 30 = 77. Better!
Can I do better? Try: Bay-Port(2), Dune-Rock(20), Dune-Mill(3), Bay-Mill(9)... that's cyclic. 
Try: Bay-Port(2), Bay-Rock(22), Cliff-Pine(9). Edge=33. Active: Port, Bay, Rock, Pine, Cliff. Interference: Bay/Port, Bay/Cliff, Cliff/Port, Cliff/Pine. Need to check chromatic number. Bay-Port, Bay-Cliff, Cliff-Port form triangle → 3 colors. Cliff-Pine adds edge. Total = 33 + 45 = 78. Worse than 77.
Try: Dune-Port(7), Dune-Rock(20), Cape-Pine(8). Edge=35. Active: Port, Dune, Rock, Pine, Cape. Interference: Dune/Port, Cape/Pine. 2 freq. Total = 35+30 = 65. Much better!
Check: Is this a tree? 3 edges, 4 nodes... wait, 5 nodes need 4 edges. Not connected - Cape not connected to rest. Need Cape-Dune(22) or Cape-Rock(17). Cape-Rock(17): edges=52, 2 freq, total=82. Or Cape-Dune(22): edges=57, total=87.
Try: Dune-Port(7), Dune-Rock(20), Dune-Pine(25). Edge=52, 4 nodes, 3 edges=tree. Interference: Dune/Port. 2 freq. Total=52+30=82. Worse than Cape approach at 77.
Try: Cape-Pine(8), Cape-Rock(17), Bay-Port(2), Bay-Dune(7)... need to connect Cape to Bay/Port side. Cape-Dune(22): total edges=49, 5 nodes need 4 edges. Interference: Bay/Port, Bay/Dune, Cape/Pine. 2 colors enough? Bay-Port, Bay-Dune edges. Cape-Pine edge. Assign Bay=1, Port=2, Dune=2, Cape=1, Pine=2, Rock=1. Total=49+30=79.
Best so far: Cape-Pine(8), Cape-Rock(17), Port-Rock(22) = 47 + 30 = 77.

**TSP:** Let me try improving the baseline tour [0,13,19,18,1,17,5,4,2,6,15,14,16,8,9,11,10,12,3,7]. 
Try moving 6 near 1,17,5: [0,13,19,18,15,12,3,7,10,11,16,8,9,2,4,5,17,6,1,14]. Let me try a cleaner approach: [0,13,19,18,15,12,10,3,7,11,16,8,9,2,4,5,17,6,1,14]. Hard to compute exactly without distances. Let me try: [0,18,19,13,7,3,12,10,11,16,8,9,2,4,5,17,6,1,14,15]. This groups bottom cities and top cities.

**CJS:** Complex, keeping baseline for now.

SUB_2: Let me carefully work on TSP and Steiner improvements.

**Steiner re-analysis:**
Current best: Cape-Pine(8), Cape-Rock(17), Port-Rock(22) = 47 + 30 = 77.
Try: Dune-Port(7), Dune-Rock(20), Cape-Pine(8), Cape-Dune(22) = 57 edges, 5 nodes, 4 edges = tree. Interference: Dune/Port, Cape/Pine. 2 freq. Total = 57+30 = 87. Worse.
Try: Bay-Port(2), Bay-Mill(9), Dune-Mill(3), Dune-Rock(20), Cliff-Pine(9). Edge=43, 7 nodes, 5 edges. Interference: Bay/Port, Bay/Cliff, Bay/Dune, Cliff/Port, Cliff/Pine, Dune/Port. Need chromatic: Bay-Port, Bay-Dune, Bay-Cliff, Cliff-Port, Cliff-Pine, Dune-Port. Bay adj to Port,Dune,Cliff. Cliff adj to Bay,Port,Pine. Port adj to Bay,Cliff,Dune. Need ≥3 colors. Total=43+45=88. Worse.
Try: Bay-Port(2), Bay-Rock(22), Cape-Pine(8). Edge=32, need to connect Cape. Cape-Rock(17)? Then Bay-Rock and Cape-Rock... 4 edges, 5 nodes. Interference: Bay/Port, Cape/Pine. 2 freq. Total=32+17=49+8=57... wait: 2+22+8+17=49. Total=49+30=79. Worse than 77.
Actually wait - is that a tree? Bay-Port, Bay-Rock, Cape-Pine, Cape-Rock. Nodes: Bay,Port,Rock,Cape,Pine. 4 edges, 5 nodes = tree. Check no cycle: Bay connects to Port and Rock. Cape connects to Pine and Rock. No cycle. Connected? Bay-Port, Bay-Rock, Cape-Rock, Cape-Pine. Yes, connected through Rock. Total = 49+30 = 79.
Try without Cape: Bay-Port(2), Bay-Rock(22), Cliff-Pine(9). Edge=33. Need connectivity: Bay-Port, Bay-Rock, Cliff-Pine. Cliff not connected to rest! Add Cliff-Rock(25): 58+interference(Bay/Port,Bay/Cliff,Cliff/Port,Cliff/Pine)→3 colors. 58+45=103. Bad.
Sticking with 77.

**TSP:** Let me compute distances for my current tour [0,13,19,18,15,12,10,3,7,11,16,8,9,2,4,5,17,6,1,14]:
0→13: √(9+64)=√73≈8.5, 13→19: √(625+0)=25, 19→18: √(49+729)=√778≈27.9, 18→15: √(324+324)=√648≈25.5, 15→12: √(576+196)=√772≈27.8, 12→10: √(1+169)=√170≈13, 10→3: √(400+64)=√464≈21.5, 3→7: √(4+361)=√365≈19.1, 7→11: √(324+1296)=√1620≈40.2, 11→16: √(225+121)=√346≈18.6, 16→8: √(1+196)=√197≈14, 8→9: √(25+121)=√146≈12.1, 9→2: √(841+4)=√845≈29.1, 2→4: √(121+289)=√410≈20.2, 4→5: √(25+0)=5, 5→17: √(36+36)=√72≈8.5, 17→6: √(289+9)=√298≈17.3, 6→1: √(36+784)=√820≈28.6, 1→14: √(1296+169)=√1465≈38.3, 14→0: √(1521+2304)=√3825≈61.8. Total≈462. Worse than baseline 447.9!

Let me try: [0,13,19,18,15,14,16,11,10,12,3,7,8,9,2,4,5,17,6,1]. Computing key segments... this should be better with 14→16→11→10→12→3→7 grouping.
